strategy: Expect two phases. Bodleian starts Balance, then Polymorphs into a Storm version once the Balance form dies, so plan for a longer fight than the 3,250 Health suggests.

Do not lean on Traps or harmful Charms in phase one. Either triggers a Cleanse Ward that wipes your setup, and Traps additionally get a Hex applied to all enemies. Build damage with blades on yourself instead.
He is Balance and takes boosted Myth (30%) and Life (80%). A Life hitter with self-blades cuts through both forms fast; avoid dumping curses onto him.
In phase one, set up with blades and burst the Balance form down in one hit rather than feeding the Trap and Charm cleanse cheats round after round.
After the Polymorph he summons two random minions on his first Storm round and re-summons when they all die. Use AoE to clear minions and hit the boss together.
Watch the round-4 Siphon: he sacrifices a minion to heal 400. Kill minions on your terms and finish him before he stalls with heals.
cheatNotes: Cast a harmful Charm and he Cleanse-Wards off all harmful Charms on himself, so Weakness-style setup is wasted.
Cast a Trap and he Cleanse-Wards off all Traps on himself, so pure trap stacking does not stick in phase one.
When the Balance form dies he does not die; he Polymorphs into a Storm version and the fight continues.
On his first round after Polymorphing he summons two random minions from the Tier 1 list.
In the Storm phase, casting a Trap makes him Cleanse off his harmful Charms and then Hex all enemies, so traps stay risky after the shift too.
Every 4th round after summoning he Siphon-Healths a minion, killing it to heal himself 400, so don't let the fight drag.
When all his minions are dead he re-summons more the next round; clear them only when you can also close out the boss.
